Replication: Kaenzig (2021)

The Macroeconomic Effects of Oil Supply News: Evidence from OPEC Announcements

Muhsin Ciftci

2026-03-25

Source: vignettes/Replications/Kaenzig2021.qmd

1 Overview

This document replicates the main empirical results of Känzig (2021) , which identifies oil supply news shocks using a high-frequency external instrument (proxy SVAR). The instrument captures exogenous variation in oil supply driven by OPEC production decisions, measured as oil futures price changes in a narrow window around OPEC announcements.

2 Setup 

library(tidyverse)
library(tidyMacro)
library(tictoc)

set_theme(ftheme_tidyMacro())

3 Data 

data("Kaenzig2021")
finaldata <- Kaenzig2021 |>
    select(Oil_Price, World_Oil_Prod, World_Oil_Inven, World_IP, US_IP, US_CPI) |>
    as.matrix()

iv_oil <- Kaenzig2021 |>
    select(iv_kanzig_final) |>
    drop_na() |>
    as.matrix()

T <- nrow(finaldata)
N <- ncol(finaldata)

varnames  <- c("Oil Price", "World Oil Prod.", "World Oil Inven.",
               "World IP", "US IP", "US CPI")
shockname <- "Oil Shock"

4 VAR Estimation 

p <- 12
c <- 1

var_result <- fVAR(finaldata, p, c)
residuals  <- var_result$residuals
sigma_full <- var_result$sigma_full

# Wold IRFs
hor  <- 48
wold <- fwoldIRF(var_result, horizon = hor)

5 Proxy SVAR: MBB Bootstrap 

tic()
result_mbb <- fbootstrapIV_mbb(
    y          = finaldata,
    var_result = var_result,
    Z          = iv_oil,
    nboot      = 1000,
    blocksize  = 0,          # auto block size
    adjustZ    = c(1, 417),
    adjustu    = c(100, 516),
    policyvar  = 1,
    horizon    = hor,
    prc        = 90
)
#> Using 3 thread(s) for parallel bootstrap computation...
toc()
#> 6.996 sec elapsed

6 Impulse Response Functions 

fplotirf_iv(
    point     = result_mbb$meanirf * 10,
    upper     = result_mbb$upper * 10,
    lower     = result_mbb$lower * 10,
    varnames  = varnames,
    shockname = shockname,
    prc       = 90,
    facet_ncol = 3
)

7 IV Identification (Manual Two-Stage)

The proxy SVAR is identified manually following the two-stage IV approach. The instrument ZtZ_t is regressed on the reduced-form residual of the policy variable (oil price) to obtain the fitted values ûp,t\hat{u}_{p,t}, which are then used to identify the structural impact vector ss.

# Align residuals and instrument to overlapping sample
u_p     <- residuals[, 1]
u_p_fin <- u_p[(length(u_p) - length(iv_oil) + 1):length(u_p)] |> as.matrix()
u_q_fin <- residuals[
    (nrow(residuals) - length(iv_oil) + 1):nrow(residuals),
    2:ncol(residuals)
] |> as.matrix()

u   <- residuals[(nrow(residuals) - length(iv_oil) + 1):nrow(residuals), ]
T1  <- nrow(u)
sigma <- (t(u) %*% u) / (T1 - 1 - p - N * p)
S     <- t(chol(sigma))

# Stage 1: regress u_p on instrument
ols1 <- fOLS(y = u_p_fin, X = iv_oil, c = 0)
uhat <- ols1$fitted_partial

# Stage 2: regress other residuals on uhat to get s_q / s_p
sq_sp <- solve(t(uhat) %*% uhat) %*% t(uhat) %*% u_q_fin

# Structural impact vector (normalised to unit oil price response)
s <- c(1, sq_sp[1], sq_sp[2], sq_sp[3], sq_sp[4], sq_sp[5])

# Structural IRFs
ivirf <- matrix(0, nrow = N, ncol = hor + 1)
for (h in 1:(hor + 1)) {
    ivirf[, h] <- wold[, , h] %*% s
}

8 Forecast Error Variance Decomposition 

fevd_result <- fevd_iv(s, S, wold, N, hor, sigma, u, T1 = T1, p = p)

fplot_vardec(
    fevd       = fevd_result$fevd_iv,
    varnames   = varnames,
    shocknames = shockname
)

9 References

Känzig, Diego R. 2021. “The Macroeconomic Effects of Oil Supply News: Evidence from OPEC Announcements.” American Economic Review 111 (4): 1092–1125. https://doi.org/10.1257/aer.20190964.
